title: DC-area forecast: Muggy overnight with a spotty shower possible
contentHtml:
Always a human at the helm: Updated around-the-clock by Capital Weather meteorologists.

Happening now: Showers and storms with heavy rain have exited the area with just a spotty shower or two possible overnight.

What’s next? Scattered showers and storms remain possible Wednesday, especially south and east of DC. Then humidity drops and much nicer weather settles in Thursday into the weekend.

Forecast in detail

Tonight: After the evening rain, skies stay mostly cloudy overnight with a slight chance of a showers. Lows are in the upper 60s to mid-70s. Winds from the southwest at 10 to 15 mph, with higher gusts at times. Confidence: Medium

Hourly Forecast | Pollen, Air & UV

Tomorrow (Wednesday): Mostly cloudy and still a little sticky, with highs in the upper 80s to near 90. Scattered showers and thunderstorms are possible again, especially during the afternoon and mainly south and east of DC. Winds from the west at 10 to 15 mph with higher gusts. Confidence: Medium

Tomorrow night: An evening shower or storm is possible before skies start to clear overnight and humidity drops considerably (dew points fall through the 60s). Lows are in the 60s. Confidence: Medium

📬 Get our forecast in your inbox

Our morning forecast email is back! It's included with every level of support — from Free to Founder — delivered 7 days a week. Sign up at: capitalweather.com/support-us

A look ahead

Thursday looks absolutely amazing, with partly to mostly skies, cooler than normal highs in the low 80s, and very low humidity (dew points dip into the low to mid-50s). Refreshing Thursday night under mostly clear skies. Lows fall into the 60s in the city, while autumn-like mid- to upper 50s are possible in our cooler suburbs — a welcome surprise for July. Confidence: Medium-High

Friday may be even better than Thursday with relatively cool highs in the upper 70s to low 80s, very low humidity, and lots of sunshine. Mostly clear and comfortable Friday night with lows mainly in the 60s, but a few lucky outer spots could still touch the 50s. Confidence: Medium-High

The final weekend of July also looks quite pleasant, with partly sunny skies and highs in the mid-80s. Saturday night remains comfortably cool, with lows in the 60s. Humidity inches up Sunday, when a late-day shower or thunderstorm becomes possible. Confidence: Medium-High

Highs next week may remain mostly in the 80s — slightly below normal — while rain chances trend somewhat above normal.

DC weather history for July 21

A rare July coastal storm in 2018 unleashed record rainfall and widespread flash flooding, capping a dramatic shift from earlier dryness.

Today’s daily digit — 3/10: Unsatisfactory all around: Warm and humid conditions fuel potential for strong to severe storms. |🤚 Your call?
The digit is a somewhat subjective rating of the day’s weather, on a 0-to-10 scale.
